workflow: Set review_decision to accepted or rejected.
Accepted entries must complete the curation block before fixture build.
Reviewed files can be passed to build_manual_fixture_from_review_candidates.py.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Energy Use Intensity (EUI) Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: EUI is total energy consumed by the building in one year divided by total gross floor area.

candidate_entries.evidence_text:
Planning the Audit
Preliminary Energy
Use Analysis:
•Baseline overview of
building's energy
performance
•Early Stage Benchmarking
•Data Sources: utility bills,
schedules (min. 1 calendar
year)
•Key Metrics:
•EUI
•ECI
•Informs depth & focus of
main audit
From ASHRAE 211 -2018, page 5
EUI: Energy Use Intensity
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑒𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦  𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑠𝑢𝑚𝑒𝑑  𝑏𝑦 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  𝑖𝑛 𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑦𝑒𝑎𝑟  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 𝑘𝐵𝑡𝑢  𝑜𝑟 𝐺𝐽)
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑔𝑟𝑜𝑠𝑠  𝑓𝑙𝑜𝑜𝑟  𝑎𝑟𝑒𝑎  𝑜𝑓 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑓𝑒𝑒𝑡  𝑜𝑟 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑚𝑒𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑠 )=𝐾𝑏𝑡𝑢
𝑆𝑄𝑓𝑡𝑜𝑟 (𝐺𝐽
𝑆𝑄𝑀)
ECI: Energy Cost Index
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑐𝑜𝑠𝑡  𝑒𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦  𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑠𝑢𝑚𝑒𝑑  𝑏𝑦 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  𝑖𝑛 𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑦𝑒𝑎𝑟  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 $)
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑔𝑟𝑜𝑠𝑠  𝑓𝑙𝑜𝑜𝑟  𝑎𝑟𝑒𝑎  𝑜𝑓 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑓𝑒𝑒𝑡  𝑜𝑟 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑚𝑒𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑠 )=$
𝑆𝑄𝑓𝑡𝑜𝑟 ($
𝑆𝑄𝑀)Free Templates

structured_payload.title: Energy Use Intensity (EUI) Definition
structured_payload.summary: EUI is total energy consumed by the building in one year divided by total gross floor area.

curation.evidence_text:
Planning the Audit
Preliminary Energy
Use Analysis:
•Baseline overview of
building's energy
performance
•Early Stage Benchmarking
•Data Sources: utility bills,
schedules (min. 1 calendar
year)
•Key Metrics:
•EUI
•ECI
•Informs depth & focus of
main audit
From ASHRAE 211 -2018, page 5
EUI: Energy Use Intensity
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑒𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦  𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑠𝑢𝑚𝑒𝑑  𝑏𝑦 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  𝑖𝑛 𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑦𝑒𝑎𝑟  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 𝑘𝐵𝑡𝑢  𝑜𝑟 𝐺𝐽)
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑔𝑟𝑜𝑠𝑠  𝑓𝑙𝑜𝑜𝑟  𝑎𝑟𝑒𝑎  𝑜𝑓 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑓𝑒𝑒𝑡  𝑜𝑟 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑚𝑒𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑠 )=𝐾𝑏𝑡𝑢
𝑆𝑄𝑓𝑡𝑜𝑟 (𝐺𝐽
𝑆𝑄𝑀)
ECI: Energy Cost Index
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑐𝑜𝑠𝑡  𝑒𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦  𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑠𝑢𝑚𝑒𝑑  𝑏𝑦 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  𝑖𝑛 𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑦𝑒𝑎𝑟  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 $)
𝑡𝑜𝑡𝑎𝑙  𝑔𝑟𝑜𝑠𝑠  𝑓𝑙𝑜𝑜𝑟  𝑎𝑟𝑒𝑎  𝑜𝑓 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑢𝑖𝑙𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔  (𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑟𝑒𝑑  𝑖𝑛 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑓𝑒𝑒𝑡  𝑜𝑟 𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑟𝑒  𝑚𝑒𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑠 )=$
𝑆𝑄𝑓𝑡𝑜𝑟 ($

structured_payload_candidate.title: ASHRAE 211-2018 Audit Levels
structured_payload_candidate.summary: ASHRAE 211-2018 establishes energy audit levels 1, 2, and 3.

candidate_entries.evidence_text:
Introduction
•The ASHRAE 211 -2018
Standard
•A complement to the
PCBEA
•Brings order into
chaos of Energy
Audits
•Establishes energy
audit levels 1, 2, 3
•Establishes
Preliminary Energy
Use Analysis Methods
•Establishes a
minimum
performance standard
for energy audits
This is the
PCBEA!
candidate_entries.judge_reason: Establishes audit levels per ASHRAE 211-2018, providing application guidance for energy audit scope.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Report Structure Executive Summary Content
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Executive summary includes brief background, scope summary, EEM summary table, and benchmarking results summary.

candidate_entries.evidence_text:
Conducting the Audit –Report Structure
1. Executive Summary
• Brief background and summary of scope
• EEM (Energy Efficiency Measures) summary table
• Summary of benchmarking results
2. Background Information
• Contact information for stakeholders
• Audit scope, methodology
• Description of the site and the building
• Historical energy consumption and costs
• Benchmarking results
• End-use analysis results
• Client motivations or mandates satisfied by this effort
3. Description of the Existing Building Systems
• Occupancy
• Building envelope
• Lighting systems
• Mechanical systems
• Security, oversight, or operational requirements impacting this
effort
• Photographs4. Energy Efficiency Measures
• No-cost/low -cost measures
• Capital investment measures
• Demand response measures
• Distributed generation/renewable energy measures
• O&M measures
• Impact of current financial market, utility incentives, federal tax or grant
support, etc.
5.
candidate_entries.judge_reason: Specifies executive summary content for audit reports, providing application guidance for audit documentation.

structured_payload.title: Report Structure Background Information Content
structured_payload.summary: Background information includes contact info, audit scope, site description, historical energy consumption, benchmarking, end-use analysis, and client motivations.

curation.evidence_text:
Conducting the Audit –Report Structure
1. Executive Summary
• Brief background and summary of scope
• EEM (Energy Efficiency Measures) summary table
• Summary of benchmarking results
2. Background Information
• Contact information for stakeholders
• Audit scope, methodology
• Description of the site and the building
• Historical energy consumption and costs
• Benchmarking results
• End-use analysis results
• Client motivations or mandates satisfied by this effort
3. Description of the Existing Building Systems
• Occupancy
• Building envelope
• Lighting systems
• Mechanical systems
• Security, oversight, or operational requirements impacting this
effort
• Photographs4. Energy Efficiency Measures
• No-cost/low -cost measures
• Capital investment measures
• Demand response measures
• Distributed generation/renewable energy measures
• O&M measures
• Impact of current financial market, utility incentives, federal tax or grant
support, etc.
5.

Use Analysis:

main audit
